mission.title: Install and Configure Argo CD Operator
mission.description: The Argo CD Operator simplifies the management of Argo CD clusters on Kubernetes. It automates the deployment and lifecycle management of Argo CD instances, making it ideal for teams adopting GitOps practices. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes operators and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Argo CD Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Argo CD Operator Helm repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add argocd-operator https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Argo CD Operator in the 'argocd' namespace using the specified Helm chart version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace argocd

helm install argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd --version 0.17.0

```

> Create a namespace for the Argo CD Operator.
> Install the operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create the CatalogSource for the Operator
steps.description:
This step creates a CatalogSource resource that points to the Argo CD Operator image.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

```

> This YAML creates the CatalogSource resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

steps.title: Create the OperatorGroup
steps.description:
This step creates an OperatorGroup resource to manage the Argo CD Operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
EOF

```

> This YAML creates the OperatorGroup resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
EOF

steps.title: Create the Subscription for the Operator
steps.description:
This step creates a Subscription resource to install the Argo CD Operator from the CatalogSource.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

```

> This YAML creates the Subscription resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Argo CD Operator installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Argo CD Operator is running successfully.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n argocd

```

> You should see the Argo CD Operator pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Argo CD Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Argo CD Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss of any Argo CD instances managed by the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall argocd-operator --namespace argocd

kubectl delete namespace argocd

```

> This command uninstalls the operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Argo CD Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any critical data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n argocd -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd --version 0.17.0

kubectl get pods -n argocd

```

> Drain the operator pod before upgrading.
> Upgrade the operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the operator pod is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n argocd -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Subscription" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OpenShift Operator Lifecycle Manager (OLM) is not installed or not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure that OLM is installed and running in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create resource: the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CatalogSource may not be available or correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Check the CatalogSource configuration and ensure the image is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The operator pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n argocd' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "CatalogSource" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The required Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) for the operator are not installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the CRDs for the Argo CD Operator are installed correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-cluster management and improved RBAC configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.16.0, ensure to review the new RBAC configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Argo CD Operator pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n argocd'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and tools installed.
